2606.15510.title: AthDGC: An Open Diachronic Greek Treebank with Indo-European Parallels
2606.15510.paper_summary: AthDGC ("Athens-PROIEL") is an open, end-to-end workflow and dataset. It is, to the best of our knowledge, the first openly licensed dependency-parsed treebank of Greek that spans eight diachronic periods, namely Archaic, Classical, Koine, Late Antique, Byzantine, Late Byzantine, Early Modern, and Modern Greek, under a single PROIEL XML 2.0 schema, with verse-level cross-alignment of the New Testament to Latin (Vulgate), Gothic (Wulfila), Old Church Slavonic (Marianus), and Classical Armenian. AthDGC builds on the PROIEL Treebank Family (Haug and Johndal 2008; Eckhoff et al. 2018), which established the schema and the Koine-Greek reference set for the project. Annotation uses the Stanford Stanza PROIEL-trained workflow; sentence-level alignment uses LaBSE, a multilingual sentence-embedding model; word-level alignment uses multilingual-BERT attention through the AwesomeAlign procedure. The v0.4 release provides curated samples and the open-source toolkit; the full annotated corpus partitions remain under v0.5 audit on the Greek national HPC. Quantitative scale, per-witness verse counts, and per-period annotated-row counts are reported in the v0.5 release notes, after the audit pass completes. Concept DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20439182.
2606.15510.paper_summary_zh: AthDGC（"Athens-PROIEL"）是一個開放的端到端工作流程和數據集。據我們所知，它是第一個公開授權的希臘語依賴解析樹庫，涵蓋八個歷時時期，即古典、古希臘、科伊內、晚古代、拜占庭、晚期拜占庭、早期現代和現代希臘語，並在單一的PROIEL XML 2.0架構下，實現新約聖經與拉丁文（武加大）、哥特文（沃爾菲拉）、古教會斯拉夫文（馬里安努斯）和古典亞美尼亞文的詩句級別交叉對齊。AthDGC基於PROIEL樹庫家族（Haug和Johndal 2008；Eckhoff等人 2018），該家族建立了該項目的架構和科伊內希臘語參考集。註釋使用斯坦福Stanza PROIEL訓練的工作流程；句子級對齊使用LaBSE，一種多語言句子嵌入模型；詞級對齊則通過AwesomeAlign程序使用多語言BERT注意力。v0.4版本提供了經過策劃的樣本和開源工具包；完整的註釋語料庫分區仍在希臘國家HPC的v0.5審計下。定量規模、每個見證的詩句計數和每個時期的註釋行計數將在v0.5版本說明中報告，審計通過完成後。概念DOI：10.5281/zenodo.20439182。
2606.15510.author: Nikolaos Lavidas et.al.
2606.15510.authors: Nikolaos Lavidas, Kiki Nikiforidou, Dag Haug, Leonid Kulikov, Vassiliki Geka, Vassileios Symeonidis, Theodoros Michalareas, Sofia Chionidi, Anastasia Tsiropina, Eleni Plakoutsi, Evangelos Argyropoulos
